verdict.answer_first: A states the answer immediately: "swallows every failure silently" and "almost never what you want"; B also starts direct but adds more framing.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder: "If no, don't catch it at all" and "empty /* ignore */ is...almost always wrong"; B surveys more alternatives.
verdict.fabrication: B overclaims with "most linters (`no-unused-vars`, `no-empty`) will flag this anyway" without evidence; both also speculate examples beyond the snippet.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to apply because it gives three concrete replacement patterns: log, narrow-and-rethrow, and fire-and-forget handling.
